editorial.body: (사설) 경기도 동탄, 기흥, 구리 등 3곳이 부동산 규제로 묶였으나 집값 상승과 풍선효과로 실효성 논란이 크다. 강력한 대출규제와 토지거래허가구역 지정에도 불구하고 시장 안정 효과는 미지수이며, 추가 정책이 요구된다. |

editorial.titleTr: Balloon Effect Spreads as Real Estate Policy Trust Plummets
summaryTr: Real Estate Regulations in Three Gyeonggi Locations Face Effectiveness Questions Due to Balloon Effect
Strong Loan Restrictions and Land Transaction Permits Designated, Yet Housing Prices Continue to Rise
Market Stabilization Effect Uncertain, Additional Policies Needed
editorial.bodyTr: Dongtan, Giheung, and Guricheon in Gyeonggi Province have been subject to real estate regulations, but their effectiveness is being questioned due to rising home prices and spillover effects. Despite strong loan restrictions and designation as land transaction permit zones, the market stabilization effect remains uncertain, and additional policy measures are needed.

editorial.titleTr: Editorial: Does the Democratic Party Think It Will Be the Majority Party Forever?
summaryTr: The Democratic Party monopolized 11 of 18 standing committee chair positions in the second half of the 22nd National Assembly.
It showed a unilateral stance of giving the remaining 7 positions to the People Power Party with the condition that they be returned if refused.
The Democratic Party's one-party dominance that disregards the opposition continues.
editorial.bodyTr: Does the Democratic Party think it will remain the majority party for a thousand years? The latter half of the 22nd National Assembly has also kicked off with the Democratic Party's unilateral dominance. After unilaterally electing 11 of the 18 standing committee chairs, the Democratic Party tossed the remaining 7 to the People Power Party with an attitude of "if you don't like it, give it back." This kind of Democratic Party dominance, with the opposition party nowhere in sight, is...

editorials.titleTr: [Commentary] Even Those Who Opposed Martial Law and Refused to "Drag Him Out" Face Insurrection Charges, Special Prosecutor Says
summaryTr: Special Prosecutor Reviews Indicting Officials Who Presented Anti-Martial Law and Refusal Opinions as Insurrectionists
Former National Security Office Chief Shin Won-sik and Ex-First Vice Director Kim Tae-hyo Under Pursuit of Arrest Warrants
Applied Charges Over Allegations of Conveying Messages Explaining Martial Law's Legitimacy to Allied Nations
editorials.bodyTr: A special prosecutor is reportedly reviewing the issuance of arrest warrants for former National Security Office Chief Shin Won-sik and former first deputy chief Kim Tae-hyo on charges of insurrection, even though they opposed martial law and refused to carry it out. The allegations concern instructing certain government agencies to convey messages to allied nations justifying the legitimacy of martial law the day after it was declared.

editorials.titleTr: Editorial: Excuse About Lower Admission Standards Rings Hollow in Military Academy Merger Plan
summaryTr: Defense Minister An Gyu-baek cited declining entrance exam scores as a reason for integrating military academies.
The explanation is that enlarging the school's scale will strengthen its capacity to nurture talented personnel.
However, critics argue it is far-fetched to use declining scores as justification for the integration.
editorials.bodyTr: The explanation that military academy integration is necessary because entrance exam scores are declining is unconvincing. Defense Minister An Kyou-baek said that entrance scores for the Army, Navy, and Air Force academies have been continuously falling, and that it is necessary to expand the schools' capacity to create a larger vessel for developing talented personnel in response to the military academy integration controversy. [뉴스1=국방부] 안규백 국방부 장관이 어제 전군 주요지휘관회의에서 “사관학교의 근본적 개혁이 시급하다”며 3군 사관학교 통합을 ‘국방부 3대 과제’의 하나로 꼽고 강력한 추진 의사를 밝혔다. 안 장관은 각 군의 전문성이 ‘칸막이’가 돼선 안 되며, 합동성을 사관학교에서부터 체질화해야 한다고 강조했다. 국방부 개혁안은 국군사관학교를 창설해 육·해·공군 사관학교 생도를 통합 선발한 뒤, 1·2학년은 공통 교육을, 3·4학년은 군별 특화 교육을 받도록 하는 방안이다. 현대전의 양상 변화 속에서 유기적인 합동 작전 능력을 키우는 것은 시대적 과제임이 분명하다. 2010년 천안함 폭침과 연평도 포격전 직후에도 군 합동성 강화 수단으로 사관학교 통합 논의가 수면 위로 올랐던 전례가 있다. 선진 강군으로 도약하기 위한 군 구조개혁 시도 자체를 나무랄 수는 없다. 그러나 군 안팎의 반대 의견을 단순한 반발로만 여기고 속도전으로 내달릴 게 아니라 면밀한 검토와 의견 수렴이 필요하다. 합동성만 추구하다 각 군의 전문성이 훼손된다면 되돌릴 수 없는 결과로 이어지기 때문이다. 더구나 12·3 비상계엄에 주도적 역할을 한 ‘육사 흔적 지우기’란 의구심도 낳고 있다. 예비역 장성이 개인 적금을 해약해 가며 일간지에 반대 광고를 내는 일들도 일찍이 보지 못했던 일들이다. 이런 의구심을 해소하지 못하고선 사관학교 통합 작업이 제대로 성과를 거두기 어렵다. 안 장관이 사관학교 개혁과 함께 3대 과제로 뽑은 전시작전통제권(전작권) 전환과 정보기관 개편 역시 마찬가지다. 북한은 핵·미사일 위협에 이어 열세였던 재래식 무기까지 현대화에 박차를 가하며 안보 위협 수위를 높이고 있다. 미·중 갈등과 북·중·러 밀착 등 한반도를 둘러싼 안보 환경 역시 급변하고 있다. 이런 시점일수록 국방부의 최우선 과제는 국방력 강화와 완벽한 안보 태세 확립에 맞춰져야 한다. 철저한 안보 환경 분석과 냉정한 우리 방위력 평가가 선행되지 않은 개혁은 안보 강화와 반대 방향의 결과로 이어질 수 있다. 국방부는 군 개혁의 목적지가 ‘강한 군대’여야 한다는 점을 명심하고, 공감대를 모으는 숙의 과정부터 밟아야 한다.
editorial.pullQuote: 합동성만 추구하다 각 군의 전문성이 훼손된다면 되돌릴 수 없는 결과로 이어지기 때문이다.
summary: 국방부, 3군 사관학교 통합을 국방개혁 3대 과제로 추진 의지 표명
합동성 강화는 필요하나 각 군 전문성 훼손 우려와 정치적 의구심 해소 필수
안보 위협 고조 시점에 철저한 분석과 공감대 형성 없는 속도전은 위험
editorial.stance: 국방개혁의 필요성은 인정하되, 충분한 검토와 사회적 공감 없이 추진되어서는 안 된다.
editorial.titleTr: [Editorial] Military Academy Merger Shouldn't Be Rushed Through
summaryTr: Defense Ministry Vows to Push Integration of Three Military Academies as Top Priority of Defense Reform
Enhanced Joint Operations Necessary, but Must Address Concerns Over Erosion of Each Service's Expertise and Dispel Political Doubts
Rushing Without Thorough Analysis and Consensus During Heightened Security Threats Poses Dangerous Risk
editorial.bodyTr:
Defense Minister Ahn Kyou-baek stated at yesterday's joint military commanders conference that "fundamental reform of the military academies is urgent," and designated the integration of the three service academies as one of the Defense Ministry's "three major tasks," expressing strong commitment to its implementation. Minister Ahn emphasized that each branch's expertise should not become a barrier, and that joint operational capabilities must be ingrained starting from the military academies.

The Defense Ministry's reform plan calls for establishing a unified Korea Military Academy to jointly recruit cadets from the Army, Navy, and Air Force academies, with first and second-year students receiving common education and third and fourth-year students receiving service-specific specialized training. There is no doubt that developing organic joint operational capabilities amid changing modern warfare is an imperative of our times. There is precedent for academy integration discussions surfacing after the 2010 sinking of the Cheonan and the Yeonpyeong shelling, both discussed as means to strengthen military jointness. Military structural reform attempts aimed at leaping forward as an advanced military power cannot be faulted in principle. However, rather than dismissing opposing views from within and outside the military as mere resistance and rushing ahead with speed, careful examination and consensus-building are necessary. If jointness is pursued at the expense of each branch's expertise, it leads to irreversible consequences.

Moreover, suspicions have arisen about "erasing Army Academy traces" following the December 3 emergency martial law declaration, given its prominent role. Retired generals liquidating personal savings accounts to publish opposition advertisements in newspapers represents an unprecedented development. Without addressing such concerns, the military academy integration project will struggle to achieve meaningful results. The same applies to the wartime operational control transition and intelligence agency reorganization that Minister Ahn designated alongside academy reform as major tasks.

North Korea is escalating security threats by modernizing conventional weapons previously held as inferior, following nuclear and missile threats. The security environment surrounding the Korean peninsula is also rapidly changing amid U.S.-China conflict and close North Korea-China-Russia alignment. In such circumstances, the Defense Ministry's top priority must focus on strengthening defense capabilities and establishing perfect security posture. Reforms undertaken without thorough security analysis and objective assessment of our defense capabilities may produce results contrary to security enhancement.

The Defense Ministry must remember that the ultimate goal of military reform should be a "strong military," and must begin by building consensus through deliberative processes.

leanScore.label: Lean conservative
leanScore.rationale: 국방력 강화와 안보 태세 확립을 최우선으로 삼으면서 군 개혁의 필요성 자체는 인정하되, 좌파 진영의 반발을 '의구심'으로 평가하고 안보 위협 고조(북핵·미중갈등)를 강조하는 보수적 안보관을 드러냄

editorial.title: Zombie firms in shadows
editorial.body: Korea needs broad-based growth, not dependence on a few winners It is no exaggeration to say that South Korea's economy is currently being propped up by the semiconductor industry. Semiconductors accounted for a record 42.3 percent of its exports in May, the highest share ever. Thanks to the explosion in demand for semiconductors driven by advances in artificial intelligence, Korea, a global semiconductor manufacturing powerhouse, is reaping substantial benefits. However, the country’s industrial sector as a whole remains mired in a slump amid deteriorating external economic conditions. The semiconductor boom is obscuring the true state of the broader economy. It will not last indefinitely. If the boom goes cold, South Korea's economic growth could take a nosedive. This is an unavoidable risk because the entire economy depends too heavily on a few sectors, including semiconductors. Only when growth is broad-based across industries can the economy remain resilient. The government should devote as much attention and support to other manufacturing industries and businesses as they do to the semiconductor sector. According to the Federation of Korean Industries' analysis of trends in marginal firms in South Korea, the United States, Britain, Germany, France and Japan from 2017 to 2025, released on Tuesday, so-called zombie companies — marginal firms unable to cover even their interest expenses with operating profit — accounted for 27.6 percent of all listed companies in South Korea last year. The figure was second only to the United States, where the share stood at 30.7 percent. The proportion of zombie companies in South Korea increased by 15.8 percentage points from 11.8 percent in 2017. Although other countries also saw an increase in the share of zombie companies during the same period, South Korea recorded the biggest increase. Its increase looks especially pronounced compared with the United States, where the share rose by the second-largest amount of 9.5 percentage points. It is true that tough external conditions, such as exchange-rate fluctuations and rising raw material prices, have increased the burden on companies across many countries. However, the sharp increase in the share of zombie companies in South Korea suggests that the country's underlying economic problems are relatively more serious. This may be the result of accumulated inefficiencies as productivity gains have failed to keep pace with rising costs and industrial restructuring has remained sluggish. The problems posed by zombie companies do not stop with the companies themselves. According to a recent Bank of Korea study, a 1 percentage point increase in the share of zombie companies in an industry lowers the investment and employment growth rates of healthy firms by 0.14 to 0.18 percentage points, with the effect lasting for two to three years. The plight of the self-employed and small business owners is no less serious than the problem of zombie companies. The self-employed's outstanding loans from financial institutions approached a record 1,100 trillion won ($705.7 billion) as of late in the first quarter of this year, while their delinquency rate rose above 2 percent, the highest level in more than a decade. The self-employed sector is also one of the pillars of the economy. South Korea has a relatively high rate of self-employment by international standards. If the self-employed sector collapses, the economy will suffer a significant shock. The Korean economy is not in particularly good shape, apart from a handful of large corporations. Korea needs to strengthen its overall economy to sustain growth in an uncertain future. Marginal companies that hurt healthy businesses need to be dealt with so they do not become a drag on the economy. Businesses with no viable future should be phased out, while those that are likely to survive and worth saving should receive support and guidance. The stellar sales and operating profits posted by the two semiconductor giants — Samsung Electronics and SK hynix — should not obscure the need to restructure failing companies. editorial.pullQuote: Only when growth is broad-based across industries can the economy remain resilient.
summary: South Korea's economy relies dangerously on semiconductors, which hit record 42.3% of exports in May.
Zombie firms unable to pay interest expenses surged to 27.6% of listed companies, the second-highest globally.
The government must support struggling sectors and self-employed workers to prevent economic collapse.
editorial.stance: South Korea must diversify its economy beyond semiconductors and address zombie firms threatening overall growth.
summaryTr: 남한 경제는 반도체에 위험할 정도로 의존하고 있으며, 5월 수출의 42.3%를 기록해 사상 최고치를 달성했다.
이자비용을 감당하지 못하는 좀비기업이 상장사의 27.6%까지 급증했으며, 이는 전 세계적으로 두 번째로 높은 수치다.
정부는 경제 붕괴를 막기 위해 어려움을 겪고 있는 산업과 자영업자들을 지원해야 한다.
editorial.bodyTr:
한국 경제는 소수 산업에 의존하지 말고 광범위한 성장이 필요하다

한국의 경제가 현재 반도체 산업에 의존해 명맥을 유지하고 있다고 해도 과언이 아니다. 5월 반도체가 수출의 42.3퍼센트를 차지해 역대 최고 기록을 세웠다. 인공지능 발전에 따른 반도체 수요 폭증의 덕분에 세계적 반도체 제조 강국인 한국은 상당한 이득을 거두고 있다. 그러나 대내외 경제 여건이 악화되면서 한국 산업 전체는 여전히 침체에 빠져 있다. 반도체 붐이 광범위한 경제의 실상을 가리고 있는 것이다. 이러한 호황이 영구적일 수는 없다. 반도체 붐이 식으면 한국의 경제 성장률은 급락할 수 있다. 반도체를 비롯한 소수 산업에 경제 전체가 과도하게 의존하고 있기 때문에 이는 피할 수 없는 위험이다. 산업 전반에 걸쳐 성장이 고르게 이루어질 때만 경제가 회복력을 유지할 수 있다. 정부는 다른 제조업 및 사업에 반도체 산업만큼 주의와 지원을 기울여야 한다.

한국경제연구원이 지난 화요일 발표한 2017년부터 2025년까지 한국, 미국, 영국, 독일, 프랑스, 일본의 한계기업 추이 분석에 따르면, 영업이익으로 이자비용도 충당하지 못하는 소위 '좀비 기업'이 지난해 한국의 상장기업 중 27.6퍼센트를 차지했다. 이는 30.7퍼센트를 기록한 미국 다음으로 높은 수치다. 한국의 좀비 기업 비중은 2017년 11.8퍼센트에서 15.8퍼센트포인트 증가했다. 다른 나라들도 같은 기간 좀비 기업 비중이 증가했지만, 한국이 가장 큰 폭의 증가를 기록했다. 2번째로 큰 폭의 증가인 미국의 9.5퍼센트포인트 증가와 비교하면 한국의 증가 추세가 특히 두드러진다.

환율 변동과 원자재 가격 상승 같은 악화된 대외 여건이 여러 나라의 기업들에 부담을 가중시킨 것은 사실이다. 그러나 한국의 좀비 기업 비중이 급증한 것은 한국의 근본적인 경제 문제가 상대적으로 더 심각함을 시사한다. 이는 생산성 증대가 상승하는 비용을 따라가지 못하고 산업 구조 조정이 지지부진하면서 누적된 비효율의 결과일 수 있다.

좀비 기업이 초래하는 문제는 기업 자체에 그치지 않는다. 한국은행의 최근 연구에 따르면 산업 내 좀비 기업 비중이 1퍼센트포인트 증가할 때 건전 기업의 투자 및 고용 성장률이 0.14~0.18퍼센트포인트 하락하며 이 효과가 2~3년간 지속된다고 한다.

자영업자와 소상공인의 현실도 좀비 기업 문제만큼 심각하다. 올해 1분기 말 기준 자영업자의 금융기관 대출금이 1,100조 원대에 달해 거의 사상 최대 수준에 근접했으며 연체율도 2퍼센트를 넘어 10년 이상 최고 수준을 기록했다. 자영업 부문도 경제의 한 축이다. 국제 기준으로 볼 때 한국의 자영업 비중은 상대적으로 높다. 자영업 부문이 붕괴되면 경제는 큰 충격을 받을 것이다.

한국 경제는 소수의 대기업을 제외하고는 특별히 나은 상태가 아니다. 한국은 불확실한 미래에서 성장을 지속하기 위해 전반적인 경제를 강화해야 한다. 건전한 기업에 해를 끼치는 한계 기업들을 처리해야 경제의 발목을 잡지 않는다. 앞날이 없는 기업은 단계적으로 퇴출하고 생존 가능성이 있으며 구원할 가치가 있는 기업들은 지원과 지도를 받아야 한다. 반도체 양대 기업인 삼성전자와 SK하이닉스가 거둔 화려한 판매량과 영업이익이 부실 기업 구조조정의 필요성을 가려서는 안 된다.

editorials.title: Zombie firms in shadows
editorials.body: Korea needs broad-based growth, not dependence on a few winners It is no exaggeration to say that South Korea's economy is currently being propped up by the semiconductor industry. Semiconductors accounted for a record 42.3 percent of its exports in May, the highest share ever. Thanks to the explosion in demand for semiconductors driven by advances in artificial intelligence, Korea, a global semiconductor manufacturing powerhouse, is reaping substantial benefits. However, the country’s industrial sector as a whole remains mired in a slump amid deteriorating external economic conditions. The semiconductor boom is obscuring the true state of the broader economy. It will not last indefinitely. If the boom goes cold, South Korea's economic growth could take a nosedive. This is an unavoidable risk because the entire economy depends too heavily on a few sectors, including semiconductors. Only when growth is broad-based across industries can the economy remain resilient. The government should devote as much attention and support to other manufacturing industries and businesses as they do to the semiconductor sector. According to the Federation of Korean Industries' analysis of trends in marginal firms in South Korea, the United States, Britain, Germany, France and Japan from 2017 to 2025, released on Tuesday, so-called zombie companies — marginal firms unable to cover even their interest expenses with operating profit — accounted for 27.6 percent of all listed companies in South Korea last year. The figure was second only to the United States, where the share stood at 30.7 percent. The proportion of zombie companies in South Korea increased by 15.8 percentage points from 11.8 percent in 2017. Although other countries also saw an increase in the share of zombie companies during the same period, South Korea recorded the biggest increase. Its increase looks especially pronounced compared with the United States, where the share rose by the second-largest amount of 9.5 percentage points. It is true that tough external conditions, such as exchange-rate fluctuations and rising raw material prices, have increased the burden on companies across many countries. However, the sharp increase in the share of zombie companies in South Korea suggests that the country's underlying economic problems are relatively more serious. This may be the result of accumulated inefficiencies as productivity gains have failed to keep pace with rising costs and industrial restructuring has remained sluggish. The problems posed by zombie companies do not stop with the companies themselves. According to a recent Bank of Korea study, a 1 percentage point increase in the share of zombie companies in an industry lowers the investment and employment growth rates of healthy firms by 0.14 to 0.18 percentage points, with the effect lasting for two to three years. The plight of the self-employed and small business owners is no less serious than the problem of zombie companies. The self-employed's outstanding loans from financial institutions approached a record 1,100 trillion won ($705.7 billion) as of late in the first quarter of this year, while their delinquency rate rose above 2 percent, the highest level in more than a decade. The self-employed sector is also one of the pillars of the economy. South Korea has a relatively high rate of self-employment by international standards. If the self-employed sector collapses, the economy will suffer a significant shock. The Korean economy is not in particularly good shape, apart from a handful of large corporations. Korea needs to strengthen its overall economy to sustain growth in an uncertain future. Marginal companies that hurt healthy businesses need to be dealt with so they do not become a drag on the economy. Businesses with no viable future should be phased out, while those that are likely to survive and worth saving should receive support and guidance. The stellar sales and operating profits posted by the two semiconductor giants — Samsung Electronics and SK hynix — should not obscure the need to restructure failing companies. summary: South Korea's economy relies dangerously on semiconductors, which hit record 42.3% of exports in May.
Zombie firms unable to pay interest expenses surged to 27.6% of listed companies, the second-highest globally.

editorials.stance: South Korea must diversify its economy beyond semiconductors and address zombie firms threatening overall growth.
summaryTr: 남한 경제는 반도체에 위험할 정도로 의존하고 있으며, 5월 수출의 42.3%를 기록해 사상 최고치를 달성했다.
이자비용을 감당하지 못하는 좀비기업이 상장사의 27.6%까지 급증했으며, 이는 전 세계적으로 두 번째로 높은 수치다.
정부는 경제 붕괴를 막기 위해 어려움을 겪고 있는 산업과 자영업자들을 지원해야 한다.

editorials.titleTr: [Editorial] Seoul Subway Free Rides Cut Back—Don't Leave Transportation Welfare Gap
summaryTr: Seoul City pushes to raise the free subway riding age from 65 to 70.
Seoul Metropolitan Council passes ordinance to support bus fares for those 70 and older.
Policy transition needed without gaps in transportation welfare.
editorials.bodyTr:
Seoul City plans to hold a public hearing this month to discuss raising the subway free-ride eligibility age from the current 65 to 70. On the 24th, the Seoul Metropolitan Council passed an ordinance to provide transportation subsidies for buses and community buses to seniors aged 70 and older. Seoul City is planning to abolish free subway rides for those aged 65 to 69 and instead provide them with transportation vouchers worth 100,000 won per month.

The rationale behind this change is straightforward: the fiscal burden of free transportation benefits for seniors has become unsustainable as the aging population grows. Free subway rides for seniors cost the city roughly 380 billion won annually, a figure that continues to rise. By raising the eligibility age, the city aims to redirect resources toward financially vulnerable elderly citizens.

However, this policy shift raises concerns about fairness and social welfare. Many people in their late 60s are still economically active or depend on regular transportation for employment. Cutting their benefits abruptly could deepen economic inequality among the elderly population.

The city should approach this matter carefully, weighing the financial realities against the welfare needs of seniors. Rather than implementing changes hastily, Seoul City should engage in thorough deliberation with residents, particularly the affected elderly, to find solutions that balance fiscal sustainability with social responsibility.

editorials.titleTr: [Editorial] Samsung and SK Must Flesh Out Semiconductor Investment Plans for Honam Region
summaryTr: The formation of the Honam semiconductor cluster, a cornerstone of the government's three mega-projects, is taking concrete shape.
Samsung and SK executives unveiled a vision for advanced industrial development in the southwestern region in Gwangju.
Concurrent advancement with the Yongin cluster must further realize semiconductor investment plans.
editorials.bodyTr:
The Honam semiconductor cluster, which is the centerpiece of the government's "three mega projects," is taking shape to be pursued simultaneously with the Yongin cluster development project. Samsung Electronics Vice Chairman Jeon Young-hyun and SK Hynix President Kwak Noh-jung presented their commitment at a "Southwest Advanced Industry Development Vision National Report" held in Gwangju, South Jeolla Province on the 30th.

The two semiconductor industry leaders' active participation in the government-led project signals strong corporate backing for the ambitious regional development initiative. The simultaneous advancement of both clusters represents a significant shift in the nation's semiconductor industry strategy, aiming to distribute advanced manufacturing capabilities across multiple regions rather than concentrating them in a single area.

Industry observers note that the dual-cluster approach could enhance the nation's semiconductor competitiveness while addressing regional development disparities. By establishing major production and research facilities in both the Honam region and Yongin, the government aims to create complementary industrial ecosystems that strengthen the overall semiconductor supply chain.

The commitment from Samsung and SK Hynix, the country's two leading semiconductor manufacturers, is expected to accelerate the project's timeline and attract additional investments from related companies and suppliers. This coordinated effort reflects the government's determination to secure long-term technological superiority in the global semiconductor market while fostering balanced regional growth.

editorial.titleTr: Growth expected at 4% but won't ease won-dollar rate forecast at 1,600 won … common people suffer most
summaryTr: South Korea has surged to fourth place globally by achieving $10 billion in monthly exports.

Structural problems have been exposed as economic growth fails to translate into improved living standards for the general public.
editorial.bodyTr: Korea has become the world's fourth nation to achieve monthly exports of $10 billion, following Germany, China, and the United States. This milestone comes on the back of a semiconductor export boom, and there are growing expectations that our economy will achieve growth close to 4 percent this year. Yet ordinary people's livelihoods show no signs of improvement. The won has also fallen to its lowest level since the financial crisis, adding to the burden of living costs. Even with high growth rates, ordinary citizens suffer..

On the 30th of last month, the ruling Democratic Party of Korea used its majority power to unilaterally elect 11 standing committee chairs, including the chairperson of the Legislation and Judiciary Committee, and members of the People Power Party submitted resignation letters en masse from these standing committees.
